exemplar.title: My learning goal is to see whether taking Business and management course in IBDP helps me to decide my future career

detailedExemplarData.title: My learning goal is to see whether taking Business and management course in IBDP helps me to decide my future career

strengths: Using WhatsApp to coordinate interviews demonstrates effective use of digital communication tools for primary research.
Logging interview durations and timestamps provides concrete evidence of your interactions and helps you track progress accurately.
Adapting your tone for different audiences shows a clear consideration of audience and strengthens your communication skills.
Recording detailed meeting notes in both first and second meetings indicates solid documentation practices, supporting the synthesis of key insights for your final product.
weaknesses: You name ATL actions such as locating, organising, and analysing, but you need to provide specific examples (e.g., âOn 10 Oct, I compared three IB resources to curate accessible summariesâ) to demonstrate their effectiveness.
Discussion of interaction skills lacks direct evidence â quote a respondentâs feedback or note how your phrasing improved their responses to show impact.
While you describe your communication strategies, you could further strengthen this section by elaborating on how these strategies improved the quality of your data or product.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You demonstrate strong digital coordination, clear audience adaptation, and thorough documentation of interviews, all of which illustrate solid ATL skill application. To raise this section to the highest level, include detailed, dated examples of each skill in action and evidence of how these practices improved your research outcomes or product quality.

strengths: Your personal connection through family history provides a compelling motivation and clear focus for your learning goal.
Thoughtfully structured interview questions strengthen the relevance of the data you will collect.
The weekly schedule table demonstrates a comprehensive timeline, effectively sequencing meetings, reviews, and drafting tasks.
A scheduled plan for interviews with specific dates enhances feasibility and credibility of your project management.
weaknesses: The learning goal is clear but not yet measurable â you need to define specific success criteria (e.g., number of interviews analysed or depth of career insights).
Product success criteria are not detailed â specify measurable features such as length, format, and content coverage to guide development and evaluation.
The plan lacks precise sequencing and resource details â add a clear timeline with deadlines and identify the tools (e.g., Google Meet, infographics software) required at each stage.
You reference SMART criteria but have not broken them down into concrete metrics and dates â for each element include specific measures and deadlines.
Milestones in your timeline are not directly linked to success criteria â align each milestone with a criterion to monitor progress and ensure on-schedule achievement.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning section is strong in personalisation and overall feasibility, with a clear schedule and focused questions. To enhance the planâs effectiveness, you need to establish measurable success criteria, detail resource use, and explicitly link each milestone to those criteria. Breaking down SMART goals with concrete metrics and timelines will make your plan fully actionable.

strengths: You show self-awareness by identifying structural issues in your draft and recognising distractions that impeded productivity.
Acknowledging the use of the Pomodoro technique demonstrates a willingness to implement and reflect on productivity strategies.
Noting your speaking techniques indicates an initial step towards metacognitive awareness of your communication style.
weaknesses: You note your speaking techniques but havenât assessed their impact on respondent engagement or clarity of their answers.
There is no evaluation of your final product against each success criterion â a self-assessment table matching outcomes to criteria with specific evidence is needed.
Although you identify poor structure, you stop short of evaluating the effectiveness of any reorganisation you attempted â reflect on one change you made and how it improved comprehension.
You mention using the Pomodoro technique but donât evaluate its effectiveness with before-and-after metrics (e.g., time-on-task improvements).
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflection section shows promising self-awareness and a willingness to adopt new strategies. However, it lacks evidence-based evaluation of those strategies and of the final product against your success criteria. Incorporating specific examples, metrics, and a structured self-assessment will transform these initial reflections into a thorough, growth-oriented analysis.

annotations.comment: Your personal connection through family history adds compelling motivation and clarity to your learning goalâexcellent linkage to personal interest.

Your learning goal is clear but not yet measurable. Define specific success criteria (e.g., number of interviews analysed or depth of career insights) so you can evaluate whether the goal has been met.
Youâve identified your product but havenât detailed success criteria for it. Specify measurable features (length, format, content coverage) to guide development and evaluation.
Your plan outlines interviews and content but lacks sequencing and resource details. Add a clear timeline with deadlines and specify tools (e.g., Google Meet, infographics software) for each stage.
You reference SMART criteria but havenât broken them down with evidence. For each element (Specific, Measurable, etc.), include concrete metrics and dates you will track.
Your interview questions are thoughtfully structured to connect classmatesâ perspectives with your learning goal. This focused questioning strengthens the relevance of your data.
You note use of speaking techniques but havenât assessed their impact. Reflect on how varying your style changed respondentsâ engagement or clarity of their answers.
The weekly schedule table demonstrates a comprehensive timeline. It effectively sequences meetings, reviews, and drafting tasks to support project progression.
To elevate this timeline, link each milestone directly to your success criteria so you can easily monitor whether each goal is achieved on schedule.
Starting with a scheduled plan for interviews and dates demonstrates feasibility and enhances the credibility of your project management.
You name ATL actions (locating, organising, analysing) but need to cite specific examples: e.g., âI compared three IB resources on 10 Oct to curate accessible summaries,â to show effectiveness.
Using WhatsApp to coordinate interviews shows effective use of digital communication tools to schedule and conduct primary research efficiently.
Logging interview durations and timestamps in a table provides concrete evidence of your interaction and helps you track progress accurately.
You discuss interaction skills but could strengthen this by quoting a respondentâs feedback or by noting how your clear phrasing improved response quality.
Adapting your tone for different audiences (informal with peers, formal with experts) demonstrates strong communication skillâyouâve considered your audience effectively.
Recording detailed meeting notes (first and second meetings) shows strong documentation and helps synthesise key insights for your final product.
You reflect on pauses to improve but lack evaluation of your final product against each success criterion. Add a self-assessment table matching outcomes to criteria with evidence.
You identify poor structure but stop short of evaluating solutions. Reflect on one reorganisation you tried and how it improved reader comprehension, supported by feedback.
You mention distractions and pomodoro technique but donât evaluate its effectiveness. Record before-and-after metrics (e.g., time on task) to demonstrate learning growth.
